programme.sub-title: Ian Irving is live from Old Trafford to explain Manchester United's new stadium plans and show exactly where the 100,000-seater ground will be located. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP31: (Subbed) PERVIS ESTUPINAN
programme.sub-title: After a nomadic start to his career, it now seems that Pervis Estupinan has found his footballing home at Brighton & Hove Albion. The Ecuadorian international takes The Big Interview to the beachfront where he talks about influence of a former professional in the family while growing up, how playing in Spain helped him to become the Premier League performer he is today and his excitement for the future playing under Fabian Hurzeler.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP41: (Subbed) ENZO MARESCA – THE ITALIAN JOB
programme.sub-title: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. PL Stories looks back at the success shared between the club and managers from Italy, while also delving into Maresca’s own unique coaching journey as he strives to emulate the achievements of those who came before him.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP34: (Subbed) CESC FABREGAS
programme.sub-title: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.
programme.desc: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP34: (Subbed) CESC FABREGAS
programme.sub-title: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.
programme.desc: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP39: (Subbed) ARSENE WENGER AND HIS MANAGERIAL JOURNEY
programme.sub-title: We explore Arsène Wenger's managerial journey before joining Arsenal, tracing his evolution from a young, innovative coach in France to a transformative force in European football. Delving into his time at Nancy, AS Monaco and Nagoya Grampus Eight, Wenger's tactical brilliance began to shape his future success. We highlight the key moments that led him to one of football's most iconic roles.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP26: THE GREATEST GAME EVER PLAYED
programme.sub-title: We gather a panel of experts to tackle a nearly impossible question: What truly is the Greatest Premier League Game Ever? Join two ex-footballers, a seasoned commentator, and a top football journalist as they lock horns in a passionate roundtable discussion. The panel revisits iconic moments, dissecting tactics, and legendary performances. Expect banter, bold claims, and maybe even a few surprises, as they strive to settle the debate—or perhaps keep it alive. Whether reliving “Fergie Time” magic, Leicester’s miracle season, or Aguero’s legendary finish, this episode celebrates the unfiltered drama that only Premier League history can offer.

programme.desc: Renowned for his lightning-fast reflexes and unwavering courage, Shay Given is one of the Premier League’s all-time greatest goalkeepers. The Irishman reflects on his most memorable moments between the posts as Newcastle’s number one.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP16: PETER SCHMEICHEL: MY PREMIER LEAGUE YEARS
programme.sub-title: Once part of a dominant Manchester United side in the 1990s, Peter Schmeichel is one of the Premier League’s most decorated goalkeepers. The Dane looks back at some of the memorable moments from his remarkable career, including a famous victory over his beloved United when he was Number 1 for their bitter city rivals.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP36: (Subbed) JOSE ANTONIO REYES
programme.sub-title: We celebrate the life and legacy of José Antonio Reyes, a footballing talent whose brilliance on the pitch captured the hearts of fans worldwide. From his early days at Sevilla to his success with Arsenal, PL Stories honours his incredible achievements. We also reflect on his tragic passing, remembering a player whose untimely loss left an indelible mark on the football world.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP34: (Subbed) CESC FABREGAS
programme.sub-title: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP32: (Subbed) THOMAS TUCHEL
programme.sub-title: As Thomas Tuchel begins his journey as England’s new manager, PL Stories dives into his journey to the top, tracing his rise from playing career to football management. We showcase his unique path, blending hard work, passion, and tactical innovation to reach the pinnacle of football.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP04: BOU v LUT 23/24
programme.sub-title: In December 2023, Bournemouth v Luton is abandoned when Tom Lockyer faced a life-threatening situation on the pitch. He survived and watched the rematch, which turned into a Premier League classic. Andoni Iraola, Lewis Cook and Antoine Semenyo tell the story.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP49: BILL FOLEY
programme.sub-title: Upon creating an American Ice Hockey team, owner Bill Foley outlandlishly claimed his team would lift the Stanley Cup in six years. His dream came true. Now, owner of AFC Bournemouth and flying in the Premier League, he’s already making bold claims for the Cherries’ future.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP32: JACK TAYLOR
programme.sub-title: To celebrate Non-League Day, this week The Big Interview travels to Portman Road to speak with Ipswich Town's, Jack Taylor. Having scored in England's top 5 leagues, Taylor talks through his career from Barnet to the Premier League. The 26-year-old also shares his views on Ipswich's season to date, the impact Kieran McKenna's had on his game as well as his hopes for the future.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP39: (Subbed) ARSENE WENGER AND HIS MANAGERIAL JOURNEY
programme.sub-title: We explore Arsène Wenger's managerial journey before joining Arsenal, tracing his evolution from a young, innovative coach in France to a transformative force in European football. Delving into his time at Nancy, AS Monaco and Nagoya Grampus Eight, Wenger's tactical brilliance began to shape his future success. We highlight the key moments that led him to one of football's most iconic roles.
programme.desc: We explore Arsène Wenger's managerial journey before joining Arsenal, tracing his evolution from a young, innovative coach in France to a transformative force in European football. Delving into his time at Nancy, AS Monaco and Nagoya Grampus Eight, Wenger's tactical brilliance began to shape his future success. We highlight the key moments that led him to one of football's most iconic roles.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP07: FERGIE v WENGER
programme.sub-title: Widely regarded as the two best managers in Premier League history, Sir Alex Ferguson and Arsene Wenger went toe to toe on the touchline on numerous occasions over the years. But what made their rivalry so fierce? We take a look at the intense clashes, memorable moments on and off the pitch and the monumental impact their duels had on English football's modern era.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP41: (Subbed) ENZO MARESCA – THE ITALIAN JOB
programme.sub-title: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. PL Stories looks back at the success shared between the club and managers from Italy, while also delving into Maresca’s own unique coaching journey as he strives to emulate the achievements of those who came before him.
programme.desc: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. PL Stories looks back at the success shared between the club and managers from Italy, while also delving into Maresca’s own unique coaching journey as he strives to emulate the achievements of those who came before him.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP32: JACK TAYLOR
programme.sub-title: To celebrate Non-League Day, this week The Big Interview travels to Portman Road to speak with Ipswich Town's, Jack Taylor. Having scored in England's top 5 leagues, Taylor talks through his career from Barnet to the Premier League. The 26-year-old also shares his views on Ipswich's season to date, the impact Kieran McKenna's had on his game as well as his hopes for the future.
programme.desc: To celebrate Non-League Day, this week The Big Interview travels to Portman Road to speak with Ipswich Town's, Jack Taylor. programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.
programme.desc: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. programme.title: PL STORIES EP29: ANDY ROBERTSON
programme.sub-title: From his humble beginnings at Queen’s Park in Scotland to becoming a key player for Liverpool, Andy Robertson’s rise is a tale of resilience, hard work, and determination. We delve in to his early struggles, his breakthrough at Hull City, and his crucial role in Liverpool's historic Premier League and Champions League triumphs. Featuring exclusive interviews with teammates, coaches, and Robertson himself, this episode celebrates his leadership, passion, and relentless spirit on and off the pitch.
programme.desc: From his humble beginnings at Queen’s Park in Scotland to becoming a key player for Liverpool, Andy Robertson’s rise is a tale of resilience, hard work, and determination. We delve in to his early struggles, his breakthrough at Hull City, and his crucial role in Liverpool's historic Premier League and Champions League triumphs. Featuring exclusive interviews with teammates, coaches, and Robertson himself, this episode celebrates his leadership, passion, and relentless spirit on and off the pitch.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP10: MICHAEL OWEN: MY BREAKTHROUGH YEARS
programme.sub-title: Michael Owen burst onto the scene for Liverpool at just seventeen years of age, and in two consecutive seasons the teenage sensation won the Premier League Golden Boot. Owen recalls those campaigns for the Reds, reliving the key moments that helped him develop into one of the greatest players of his generation.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP37: (Subbed) NANI
programme.sub-title: A player of flair and flamboyance for Sir Alex Ferguson’s Manchester United, the story of Nani from the streets of Lisbon is one of defying the odds. Nani retraces his footsteps across Portugal’s capital, visiting old clubs and former coaches, the neighbourhood he learnt to survive in, as well as new ventures afoot.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP51: CHAMPIONS SPECIAL
programme.sub-title: Season 2023 - 2024 has arguably been the greatest since the birth of the Premier League in 1992. Three elite teams racing to the finish line. Each of them believing this season will end in glory. The script of the story is captivating. Manchester City going for an historic fourth consecutive Premier League title. Arsenal looking for their first title in twenty years following their late season collapse in 2023. Liverpool looking to give their manager Jurgen Klopp the perfect send off as he says good-bye to Anfield after nine years. With the help of leading celebrity fans, the managers and senior players each twist and turn of the season is highlighted. The narrative of the season is like no other. The Champions Story 2023 - 2024 tells a very special story.
programme.desc: Season 2023 - 2024 has arguably been the greatest since the birth of the Premier League in 1992. Three elite teams racing to the finish line. Each of them believing this season will end in glory. The script of the story is captivating. Manchester City going for an historic fourth consecutive Premier League title. Arsenal looking for their first title in twenty years following their late season collapse in 2023. Liverpool looking to give their manager Jurgen Klopp the perfect send off as he says good-bye to Anfield after nine years. With the help of leading celebrity fans, the managers and senior players each twist and turn of the season is highlighted. The narrative of the season is like no other. The Champions Story 2023 - 2024 tells a very special story.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP05: LEICESTER CITY - CHAMPIONS 15/16
programme.sub-title: Leicester City's Premier League triumph is one of the greatest underdog stories in the history of sport. Wes Morgan and Marc Albrighton recount the pivotal moments that defined their remarkable journey to the title.
